steps.title: Identify the sentence boundary
steps.content: The text describes two minerals. The first sentence is about krotite (classified as transparent). The second sentence is about beryl, beginning with a participial phrase: Allowing only some light to pass through, which modifies beryl. So a sentence break is needed after transparent.

Choice A uses a period and capitalizes Allowing, correctly ending the krotite sentence and starting a new sentence whose participial phrase modifies beryl. Choice B has no punctuation (run-on), C has only a comma (comma splice between independent clauses), and D inserts and in a way that doesn't connect the two clauses correctly. That matches choice A — A is correct.
steps.title: Apply the boundary punctuation
